top_bullish: ‘Korean markets are like small caps’, says Capitalmind Mutual Fund CEO Deepak Shenoy; Kospi jumps 5%
Top Gainers & Losers on June 25: HFCL, Tata Motors, IndiGo, CarTrade Tech, Paytm, UNO Minda among top gainers
Sensex drops 700 points from day's high to close flat, Nifty 50 ends near 24,050; mid, small-caps underperform
top_bearish: Muthoot Finance, Manappuram Finance shares decline up to 3.5% as gold prices hit multi-month lows
New India Assurance: FY27 profit may get capital gains boost from NSE stake sale, but watch underwriting loss
Aviation stocks fly high: IndiGo, SpiceJet shares jump up to 6% as crude oil prices slip to below pre-US-Iran war levels

articles.title: ‘Korean markets are like small caps’, says Capitalmind Mutual Fund CEO Deepak Shenoy; Kospi jumps 5%
articles.summary: Investor sentiment in South Korea shifted amid falling oil prices and positive news from US-Iran talks. However, proposed taxes on unrealised gains introduced uncertainty, contributing to significant market volatility and sharp fluctuations for major technology stocks.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 25: HFCL, Tata Motors, IndiGo, CarTrade Tech, Paytm, UNO Minda among top gainers
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 retreated 240 points from the day’s high to settle at 24,056, up 0.14% from the previous close. Meanwhile, the Sensex erased 707 points from its intraday high to conclude the session with a similar gain of 0.14%, closing at 77,098.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sensex drops 700 points from day's high to close flat, Nifty 50 ends near 24,050; mid, small-caps underperform
articles.summary: Stock market today: The Sensex dropped 703 points from its intraday high of 77,803.18 to end at 77,100.47. The Nifty 50 hit an intraday high of 24,261.60 during the session but closed at 24,056, up 34 points, or 0.14%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Muthoot Finance, Manappuram Finance shares decline up to 3.5% as gold prices hit multi-month lows
articles.summary: Gold loan NBFCs, including Muthoot and Manappuram, have seen significant stock declines related to falling gold prices and heightened US Federal Reserve rate hike expectations.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 up just 2% in last 2 years. Could a bull run be next amid falling oil prices, easing geopolitical risks?
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 has stagnated with just a 2% return over the last two years. With oil prices dropping and geopolitical tensions easing, experts speculate that a potential bull run could be imminent if economic indicators remain strong.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advik Laboratories’ rights issue stayed by Delhi High Court
articles.summary: Advik Laboratories has been restrained from progressing with key stages of the rights issue process, including regulatory filings, circulation of offer documents, acceptance of subscriptions, and allotment of shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: New India Assurance: FY27 profit may get capital gains boost from NSE stake sale, but watch underwriting loss
articles.summary: NIACL's stock surged nearly 30% after the NSE's IPO prospectus revealed NIACL as a selling shareholder. Despite profit growth, NIACL faces underwriting losses and a high combined ratio, leading to a lower valuation multiple compared to competitors.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver price tanks 14% in a week leading to 9% decline in Hindustan Zinc share price. Is worst over for white metal?
articles.summary: Hindustan Zinc shares dropped 4% to ₹531.1 as silver prices continued to decline, impacted by a stronger US dollar and rising bond yields. Silver fell 14% this week, exacerbated by diminished geopolitical tensions and expectations of higher US interest rates affecting investor demand.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Aastha Spintex IPO: Price band set at ₹125-136 per share; check key dates, issue details
articles.summary: Aastha Spintex IPO offers shares at ₹125 to ₹136, open for subscription from June 29 to July 1. The offering allocates shares to QIBs, NIIs, and retail investors, aiming to raise ₹170 crore for acquisitions and working capital.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Silver price crashes over 50% from peak, falls below $60 an ounce; Can MCX silver rate fall below ₹2,00,000 per kg?
articles.summary: Silver prices are now down approximately 53% from that peak. The sharp correction has erased more than half of silver’s value from its all-time high of $121.78 per ounce recorded in January 2026.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crazy Snacks IPO day 1: GMP, subscription status, price band, other details of BSE SME IPO
articles.summary: Crazy Snacks IPO is a combination of fresh issue of 60 lakh shares and offer for sale (OFS) of 15 lakh shares. With a price band of ₹39 to ₹42 per share, the company wants to raise ₹25.20 crore via the fresh sale of shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Atharva Polyplast IPO: BSE SME declares price band at ₹55 to ₹60. GMP, date, other details in 10 points
articles.summary: The company has fixed the price band of ₹55 to ₹60 per share. The shares of Atharva Polyplast IPO is trading at a premium of ₹10 in the grey market
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sterlite Technologies share price jumps 5% on launch of ₹45,450 crore QIP; sets floor price at ₹613.69
articles.summary: Sterlite Technologies shares rose 5% to ₹613.35 as the company initiated a QIP to bolster its capital base. The floor price was set at ₹613.69, and the issue opens after regulatory approvals on June 24, 2026.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Nagaraj Shetti recommends Ashok Leyland, Mahindra & Mahindra Financial shares to buy in the short-term
articles.summary: The Indian stock market showed gains on Thursday, supported by declining crude oil prices. The Nifty 50 rose 0.70% and the BSE Sensex went up 0.68%. While realty and auto sectors performed well, metal and media stocks faced profit-booking pressure.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: FPIs sell ₹63,450 crore of Indian equities in June first half; financials, oil & gas stocks see highest outflows
articles.summary: FPI exodus: The Financial Services sector recorded the highest FPI selling during the first half of June, with net outflows of ₹11,263 crore. The Oil, Gas & Consumable Fuels sector followed closely, witnessing outflows of ₹10,488 crore, according to NSDL data.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Vodafone Idea, Motherson Sumi Wiring, YES Bank among most traded stocks on NSE today, 25 June 2026
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea, Motherson Sumi Wiring India, Tata Silver Exchange Traded Fund, Tata Gold Exchange Traded Fund, Nippon India Silver ETF, YES Bank, Suzlon Energy, and IRFC were among the most traded stocks on the NSE.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Aviation stocks fly high: IndiGo, SpiceJet shares jump up to 6% as crude oil prices slip to below pre-US-Iran war levels
articles.summary: Airline shares rose on June 25 as crude oil prices hit new lows, with SpiceJet up 5.5% and InterGlobe Aviation gaining 4.7%. The decline in oil prices, driven by eased supply concerns, improves profit margins for airlines, with forecasts suggesting oil could stabilize around $67 per barrel.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Direct impact of poor rains on inflation and growth is weakening. The indirect hit is harder to ignore.
articles.summary: As monsoon falters due to El Niño, the ripple effects on prices and demand matter more than the direct hit on India's inflation and GDP growth rates.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stock market holiday: Are BSE and NSE closed for trading tomorrow for Muharram? Check remaining holidays for 2026
articles.summary: The Indian stock market will be closed on June 26 for Muharram, suspending trading on the NSE and BSE. After this holiday, the next market closure is on September 14 for Ganesh Chaturthi, with 16 holidays scheduled for 2026, including weekends.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO Day 3: Here's GMP, subscription status, other key details - apply or not?
articles.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO: Shares of Waterways Leisure Tourism are attracting a modest grey market premium (GMP) in the unofficial market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CSM Technologies IPO Day 2: GMP, subscription status, price band, review, other key details. Should you subscribe?
articles.summary: CSM Technologies IPO price band has been set at ₹107 to ₹113 per share. At the upper-end of the IPO price band, the company aims to raise ₹145.78 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely a fresh issue of 1.29 crore equity shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger stock HFCL share price hits 5% upper circuit following broader gains on Dalal Street; up 200% in 3 months
articles.summary: HFCL has been among the market's standout performers in recent months. The stock touched a 52-week high of ₹220.10 on June 22, 2026, after recovering sharply from its 52-week low of ₹59.83 recorded in January 2026.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Dalmia Bharat share price gains as Goldman Sachs upgrades to ‘Buy’; sees 17% upside
articles.summary: Dalmia Bharat share price rose nearly 2% after Goldman Sachs upgraded it to a Buy. The target price was revised to ₹2,020, indicating a 17% upside. Concerns exist over energy costs, demand outlook, and capacity additions, but easing tensions in the West Asia conflict support a positive view.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: South Korean AI chip stock SK Hynix share price surges 13% after unveiling $29 billion US listing plan
articles.summary: SK Hynix shares surged nearly 13% after announcing plans to raise $29.4 billion through a US listing, fueling a rally in South Korea's semiconductor sector. Investors anticipate strong AI demand, leading to significant stock price increases over various time frames.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty Auto jumps over 2%; Maruti, TVS Motor, Ashok Leyland, M&M among top gainers. Why are auto stocks rising?
articles.summary: On Thursday morning, major auto stocks, such as Maruti, TVS Motor, Ashok Leyland, Mahindra and Mahindra, Hero MotoCorp, and Eicher Motors, rose more than 2% each.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Delhivery share sale: Logistics stock falls over 2% after Alpha Wave Ventures sells entire 1.93% stake
articles.summary: Alpha Wave Ventures LP sold its entire 1.93% stake in Delhivery in two tranches on June 24. The Delhivery stake sale by Alpha Wave Ventures LP comes a few days after venture capital firm Nexus Venture Partners offloaded its 1.97% stake in Delhivery.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Avience Biomedicals share price lists at 90% premium over the IPO price
articles.summary: Avience Biomedicals share price opened at ₹395.20 apiece on NSE SME. The SME stock listed at 90% premium over the IPO price of ₹208.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: IRFC OFS opens for retail investors today. Should you participate?
articles.summary: Non-retail investors fully subscribed to the IRFC OFS on Wednesday, placing bids for 1.86 times the shares reserved for their category. Following the strong response, the Centre has decided to exercise the greenshoe option.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying HDFC Bank, Emcure Pharma shares on 25 June
articles.summary: Indian equity benchmarks opened higher today, driven by gains in Asian markets and easing crude oil prices. The Nifty 50 rose 0.43%, and market breadth remained positive with 15 out of 16 major indices in the green, showcasing investor confidence amid improving global sentiment.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: 'There is no quick way to make money': Zerodha's Nithin Kamath issues warning that could save investors millions
articles.summary: Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath highlights the persistence of pyramid schemes in India, with many investors misled by promises of easy profits. He cautions that this mindset can lead to significant financial losses, urging individuals to be wary of recruitment-based opportunities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold rate falls on MCX on a stronger dollar amid rising expectations of US Fed rate hikes; what should investors do?
articles.summary: Gold rate on the MCX declined in the morning on Thursday, 25 June, due to a stronger dollar. MCX gold August futures were 0.37% down at ₹1,40,749 per 10 grams, while MCX silver July futures were 1.07% down at ₹2,10,801 per kg around 9:10 am.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 37 paise higher at 94.30 against US dollar
articles.summary: The rupee rose by 37 paise to 94.30 against the US dollar, driven by falling crude oil prices and likely RBI intervention, despite pressure from strong dollar demand and expectations of higher US interest rates.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Where is gold headed if the war ends?
articles.summary: Gold, as a safe-haven investment, was clearly on investors' and traders' minds following the outbreak of war in West Asia. And sure enough, the price of gold initially went up, but then came the correction.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy for short term: PVR INOX among 3 stocks Amol Athawale of Kotak Securities recommends for next 1-2 weeks
articles.summary: Amol Athawale from Kotak Securities identifies three stocks for the next 1-2 weeks, including PVR INOX and Aadhar Housing Finance, with strong technical indicators and favourable market conditions.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Advit Jewels IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed 200.67x so far. GMP hints 38% listing pop. Should you apply or not?
articles.summary: Advit Jewels IPO saw strong demand, with subscriptions 44.16 times oversubscribed by diverse investors. The issue opens on 23 June and closes on 25 June, targeting a 1 July debut at an estimated 37.68% premium to the issue price of ₹138.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Weak core output, Russian crude imports, AI edge
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: For better outcomes, Centre nudges states on focused capex for ₹2 tn loan scheme
articles.summary: The DoE has asked states and UTs to limit their proposals under its SASCI scheme for capex to their choice of maximum five priority sectors in FY27. The move marks a shift from simply encouraging higher capex to ensuring more concentrated investments that deliver measurable development outcomes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
articles.summary: Driven by a ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Businesses embrace GST, but seek more reforms: Deloitte survey
articles.summary: The findings come at a time when GST collections continue to be robust. In May, the Centre and states together collected ₹1.94 trillion in gross GST revenue, before adjusting for refunds, up 3.2% from ₹1.88 trillion mopped up a year ago.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Why India’s consumers may stay cautious despite easing oil prices
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices offer hope, but delayed rains, food inflation and higher living costs continue to weaken consumer confidence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Millets, check dams and emergency plans: How India is preparing for dry days
articles.summary: Low monsoon rainfall is significant, as the season accounts for about 70% of India's annual rainfall. The rural economy, which relies heavily on agriculture, remains an important driver of economic growth.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RBI allows banks to give loans for FCNR deposits
articles.summary: On 5 June, RBI said it will absorb the foreign exchange hedging cost on fresh  CNR (B), deposits, creating an unusual opportunity for overseas Indians
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Moving decisively towards finalizing trade deal with India, says US Ambassador Gor
articles.summary: India and the US are moving closer to finalizing a bilateral trade agreement, with commerce minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer holding talks in New Delhi. Negotiations focus on tariffs, market access and digital trade ahead of a key US tariff deadline in July.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India's private sector output moderates further in June
articles.summary: June saw slower growth in new orders, international sales, employment and business activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will Singapore remain India's export star even after Iran war ends?
articles.summary: West Asian geopolitical tensions have driven a surge in India’s exports to Singapore, utilizing its robust transshipment infrastructure to diversify from traditional hubs. Will this continue?
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt plans subsidized tomato sales to cool price surge
articles.summary: In several local retail markets, tomatoes are currently being sold at ₹60-70 per kg. Retail tomato prices have jumped in India due to delays in arrivals and weather-related disruptions in producing regions.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade chief to visit India for interim deal discussions
articles.summary: The visit comes amid efforts by India and the US to deepen economic ties and address market access issues through a comprehensive trade pact.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI governor asks banks to treat MSMEs as long-term partners
articles.summary: The RBI governor asked financial institutions to make greater use of India's digital public infrastructure
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI flags risks if West Asia peace deal fails, says India remains strong
articles.summary: RBI's assessment comes amid easing tensions between the US and Iran, which has helped moderate concerns around energy supply disruptions and global trade routes
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: India's core sector growth hits seven-month low as Iran war bites
articles.summary: Given the tepid core sector output in May 2026, India's IIP growth is likely to weaken to 2-3% in the month from 4.9% in April 2026, according to an analyst.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gen Z is out-earning millennials, shows data; but has inflation already closed the gap?
articles.summary: New research shows Gen Z workers are out-earning millennials at the same age — but with soaring rents in the UK, US, and India, higher wages may not mean better lives. Here's what the data really shows.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: From village adulteration to organized food crime: The evolution of India’s fake milk industry
articles.summary: Evidence is emerging that in some northern states and Gujarat people are being sold fake milk that looks and tastes like the real thing. This deep dive looks at the growing sophistry in adulterating milk and how consumers can protect themselves.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The benefits and risks of RBI’s policy on foreign currency NRI deposits
articles.summary: To shore up the rupee, the government is looking at NRIs to make foreign currency deposits in Indian banks. This has implications for India’s external debt management.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India, Iran look to soon resume talks to get Chabahar Port plan back on track
articles.summary: India and Iran may resume discussions on restoring normal operations at the strategic Chabahar Port and reviving plans to expand the facility, as geopolitical conditions in the region show signs of stabilising, according to two people aware of the matter.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Young Punjab kids ‘rich for 4 yrs after selling their lands… then broke’: World Bank's Ajay Banga on urban agri economy
articles.summary: World Bank President Ajay Banga pointed out challenges in urban and agricultural economies, particularly in Punjab, where youth selling farmland quickly spend their earnings, leading to financial instability.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Views, virality and CJP protest: How India's creator economy found a new stage at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: A May 2025 report, ‘From Content to Commerce: Mapping India’s Creator Economy’ by BCG, said that with over 2–2.5 million monetised content creators influencing more than $350–400 billion in consumer spending, the ecosystem is projected to drive $1 trillion+ in creator-influenced consumption by 2030.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: State debt concerns, WPI inflation, NSE’s IPO debut
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Development finance should be used strategically to improve governance, service delivery in Northeast: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman said that externally aided project funding to India's Northeast has risen nearly eightfold to ₹76,000 crore since 2014, and urged states to use such finance to strengthen governance and service delivery.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AI boom shields Asia from West Asia shock, but risks mount for India and region: Moody’s Analytics
articles.summary: Moody's Analytics has said that AI-led exports and investment have helped Asia-Pacific weather the fallout from the West Asia conflict, but higher oil prices, inflation, trade tensions and weather-related disruptions could slow growth in the years ahead.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Watchful and wary: Inside MPC's unanimous decision to hold rates
articles.summary: At its 3-5 June meeting, the Monetary Policy Committee kept the key repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, while acknowledging that there were considerable risks to its assessments of inflation and growth.
articles.label: BEARISH
